nodes.label: Input Aggregation Gate
nodes.description: AND gate that requires Observation, Rules, human interaction, and forecast improvement criteria to all be present before the causal chain can be fully instantiated. Enforces that the causal chain node is not triggered by partial inputs.

nodes.label: Forecast Confidence Level
nodes.description: A measurable state reflecting the confidence score of the produced Causal Forecast, enabling downstream evaluation and feedback loops.

nodes.label: Rules Refinement Task
nodes.description: A task that updates and refines the Rules blackbox based on forecast improvement criteria, closing the loop between evaluation concepts and the rule mechanism.

nodes.label: Data Source Change
nodes.description: An external event representing a change in the underlying data source or signal, which perturbs the observation process and can degrade or shift causal chain outputs.

nodes.label: Forecast Acceptance Decision
nodes.description: A decision point evaluating whether the Causal Forecast meets acceptance thresholds: Branch A — accept and publish forecast; Branch B — reject and trigger human interaction for revision.

summaries.headline: Raw observations, rules, and human judgment are funneled through a gated causal engine to produce a forecast, which is then evaluated, refined in a feedback loop, and either published or sent back for revision.

data.headline: Raw observations, rules, and human judgment are funneled through a gated causal engine to produce a forecast, which is then evaluated, refined in a feedback loop, and either published or sent back for revision.

goal.label: Causal Forecast
goal.plain: Success means producing a reliable, accepted causal forecast that meets quality thresholds and can be published with confidence.

critical_path.label: Observation
critical_path.plain: The process begins with observing and filtering incoming signals or data — this is the primary trigger that sets everything in motion.

critical_path.label: Input Aggregation Gate
critical_path.plain: Before any analysis runs, all four required inputs — observations, rules, human interaction, and improvement criteria — must be present. Nothing proceeds on partial information.

critical_path.label: causal chain
critical_path.plain: Once all inputs are confirmed, the core analytical engine processes them to build a structured causal explanation of what is happening and why.

critical_path.label: Causal Forecast
critical_path.plain: The causal chain produces the forecast — the central output of the entire system.

critical_path.label: Forecast Acceptance Decision
critical_path.plain: The forecast is evaluated against acceptance thresholds: if it passes, it is published; if it fails, it is routed back to humans for revision.

critical_path.label: Forecast Confidence Level
critical_path.plain: The forecast generates a measurable confidence score that feeds into evaluation and informs whether refinement is needed.

tasks.label: Rules Refinement Task
tasks.plain: When forecast improvement criteria are available, this task updates and sharpens the rules that power the causal engine — closing the feedback loop so the system learns from past forecast quality.

decisions.label: Input Aggregation Gate
decisions.plain: An AND-gate that enforces a hard precondition: the causal chain will not run unless observations, rules, human interaction, and forecast improvement criteria are all simultaneously present.

branches.label: All inputs present
branches.outcome: The causal chain is activated and proceeds to generate a forecast.

branches.label: One or more inputs missing
branches.outcome: The process is blocked and no forecast is produced until all inputs are available.

decisions.label: Forecast Acceptance Decision
decisions.plain: A quality checkpoint that determines whether the produced forecast is good enough to publish or must be revised.

branches.label: Branch A — Accept
branches.outcome: The forecast meets thresholds and is published.

branches.label: Branch B — Reject
branches.outcome: The forecast is sent back to human reviewers, who re-enter the process and trigger another iteration of the causal chain.

risks.label: Data Source Change
risks.plain: An unexpected change in the underlying data feed can disrupt the observation step, feeding corrupted or shifted signals into the entire causal chain and degrading forecast quality without any obvious warning.

risks.label: Causal chain (blackbox)
risks.plain: The core analytical engine is a blackbox — its internal logic is not transparent. If it malfunctions or produces biased outputs, it may be difficult to diagnose or correct, and those errors will flow directly into the forecast.

risks.label: Rules (blackbox)
risks.plain: The rules mechanism is also opaque. Stale or incorrect rules will silently amplify errors through the causal chain; without visibility into the rules, diagnosing a bad forecast becomes much harder.

risks.label: Feedback loop: Causal Forecast → Observation
risks.plain: The forecast amplifies back into the observation step, creating a closed loop. If the forecast is wrong, it could bias future observations, causing the system to reinforce its own errors over time.

open_questions: What are the specific 'forecast improvement criteria' and how are they measured or updated? This concept drives both the gate and the rules refinement task but its definition is not specified.
What exact thresholds does the Forecast Acceptance Decision use, and who sets them? Without clear criteria, the accept/reject branching is subjective and inconsistent.
How many rejection-and-revision cycles are allowed before the process is escalated or abandoned? The feedback loop between rejection and human interaction could cycle indefinitely.
What happens inside the causal chain blackbox — is there a mechanism to audit or explain its outputs, especially when forecasts are rejected?
